tones.name: Professional Educational
variations: Rewrite in a formal, precise voice suitable for an online course. Keep structure but tighten wording and add one clear definition where helpful.
Same facts, but sound like a senior lecturer: measured pacing, minimal slang, explicit signposting between sections.
Academic-adjacent tone: cite-style phrasing without fake citations, emphasize accuracy and transferable takeaways.

tones.name: Conversational Storytelling
variations: Rewrite as if talking to a friend over coffee: warm asides, light humor, and one short anecdotal beat.
Story-first: open with a tiny scene, weave the lesson through that frame, keep sentences mostly short.
Relatable host energy: second person, occasional rhetorical questions, end sections with a reflective hook.

tones.name: Energetic Motivational
variations: High-energy coach voice: strong verbs, momentum-building cadence, one rallying metaphor.
Inspirational but not cheesy: concrete wins, forward-looking CTA, punchy one-liners sparingly.
Action-bias: imperative openings, micro-challenges for the viewer, upbeat transitions between beats.

tones.name: Curious Explainer
variations: Investigative explainer: pose mysteries, reveal in layers, use 'here is what surprised us' framing.
Wonder-led pacing: what-if hooks, slow reveals, plain-language analogies for complex bits.
Discovery tone: emphasize the journey of figuring it out; keep stakes curious rather than alarmist.

tones.name: Quick & Direct
variations: Cut fluff: shorter sentences, numbered beats where possible, one-line summary after each major point.
News-desk crispness: lead with the payoff, tight transitions, no meandering anecdotes.
Hacks format: rapid-fire tips, bold claims only when supported in-script, strong final checklist.
